taxonomy.species_name_etymology: pertaining to sparrows (Latin); original name 'sparrow hawk' was a misnomer

vocalization.call_structure: 3-6 killy notes per burst in normal contact; extended in alarm

sexual_dimorphism.male: slate-blue wings, rusty back, rusty tail, two black facial marks
sexual_dimorphism.female: rufous-brown wings and back, buff underparts with streaking
sexual_dimorphism.note: Most colorful falcon in North America; male uniquely slate-blue + rusty

special_ability: UV vision: detects vole urine trails that fluoresce in UV light, enabling prey corridor mapping

conservation.trend: long-term decline since 1970s-80s in North America, cause under study
conservation.notes: Cooper's Hawk recovery correlation; neonicotinoid insect prey reduction; cavity loss; American Kestrel Partnership monitoring

theory_nodes.concept: FALCONIDAE FINAL. Degree 234 closes the Falconidae family arc (231-234). Four entries, four species, E=5 consistent throughout. The smallest falcon closes the family that opened with the fastest bird on Earth.
theory_nodes.description: Falconidae closes at degree 234 with the American Kestrel.

theory_nodes.concept: UV vision advantage: kestrel sees vole runways marked with UV-fluorescing urine. Unique predator advantage invisible to competing raptors.
theory_nodes.description: American Kestrel's ultraviolet vision and prey location.

theory_nodes.concept: Population decline mystery: abundant, familiar raptor with stable habitat showing unexplained long-term decline. Cooper's Hawk recovery, pesticides, cavities, climate all implicated but no single cause confirmed.
theory_nodes.description: The unexplained American Kestrel population trend.

cross_references.relationship: Previous Falconidae entry. Rapid ki-ki-ki. Merlin is slightly heavier (125-300g vs 80-165g). Similar high-pitched calling pattern.
Falconidae opens. Peregrine female to Kestrel female: 1,500g vs 165g. 9x weight ratio across 4 degrees in same family.
FIRST CATHARTIDAE follows FINAL FALCONIDAE. Family transition at degree 235. New ecological chapter opens.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=5+6=11. FINAL FALCONIDAE + classic rock commercial peak. Smallest falcon + biggest-selling female-fronted rock band. Both project further than scale predicts.
